Operating ever since 1885.

🔍 '''Level 9.12'''
----

{{Class 0|safety=Safe|security=Stable|entity=Devoid of Entities}} Level 9.12 is a sublevel of [[Level 9]]. == Description == {{Blurimage|caption=Repeating houses in Level 9.12.|file=Screenshot 2025-04-04 082056-434-1-3.png}} Level 9.12 is a large network of suburbs in the Dreamcore style. Each house is a plethora of different colors, from pastel red to dark purple. The temperature within Level 9.12 is quite cool, reaching approximately 59 degrees Fahrenheit (15 degrees Celsius). The sky contains no clouds, and a large light source acts as the "sun" for the level. Level 9.12 has a day-light cycle of 12 hours, with 6 hours of daytime and 6 hours of nighttime. During the nighttime, a smaller light source represents the "moon". Level 9.12 does not harbor any non euclidean properties, making it easy to navigate. == Entities == There are no entities here. == Colonies and Outposts == There are none. == Entrances and Exits == === Entrances === The only known way to enter this level is through Level 9 by entering a street named "Yonder Way" === Exits === The level can be exited by entering an out of place farmhouse, leading to [[Level 10]].
'''> Open Author Information'''
'''> Close Author Information'''

{| class="fandom-table" | style="border: 2px solid white; color: white;"|'''From:''' Zachary G. (zachG@backos.bri) |- | style="border: 2px solid white; color:white;"|'''To:''' Hansel Northfields (MEGnorthfields19@meg.bri) |- | style="border: 2px solid white; color:white;"|'''Subject:''' '''Help''' |}
----
Hey, sorry to message you at this hour. I noticed something odd on the database. I vividly remember a Level 9 sublevel, Level 9.12, the Dreamcore Suburbs. It was on the database a few days ago, but then it just... vanished. Like it just got wiped. At first, I thought it was because of quality standards, but then I checked and the page had no deletion log. What the fuck is going on here? Also, before you push me off as crazy, I nabbed an archive from when the page was in development. It's since been expanded since then, so it is pretty low quality. https://backosarchives.back/the_dreamcore_suburbs -Zach G

Huh, that is pretty weird. The name rings a bell, but it's hard to pinpoint. I don't remember a Level 9.12 on the database, but it's dated from back in August, so it had to be official. Imma speak to higher ups about this, something about it seems odd to me. - Hansel Northfields

'''Re:''' Alright so I talked to the higher ups here at Base Alpha about the whole Level 9.12 situation. All of them told me they did not remember a Level 9.12, or any sort of dreamcore suburb level in general. Even after I showed them the archive you sent me, they still denied it. I'm afraid there's nothing I can do for you. Convincing these people that Level 9.12 has existed on the database is an uphill battle. Besides, there's no benefit to doing this other to please you, so it is not even worth it. Still, we will be in touch and I'll notify you if there's any update with the Level 9.12 situation. - Hansel Northfields
{{Underline|FFFFFF}}
=
'''Base Alpha: The Compass Point'''
=
{{Underline|808080}}


"Yo look what I found"


"Huh?"



"So you know the recording software that Zach put on his computer to record every single audio log?"


"Yea? so-"



"Well turns out the dude was a wannabe programmer and made another software to record whatever he wants to capture within his computer. It sends a snapshot to a particular folder, which was then backed up to the Database cloud as a whole, and within that folder, we found some crazy shit."


"Like?"



"We found this trail of emails between him and Northfields."


"Northfields? You mean the same guy who accidentally noclipped to [[Level 51]] and went absolutely insane in there?"



"Yep. THAT Northfields"


"He was talking to him about a random database level called "Level 9.12", which many of us remember him babbling his mouth about right before he noclipped. The thing is, Level 9.12 does not exist on the current version of the database. However, Zach showed an archive in the message as the page was being developed. I believe that's where Northfields was heading to before meeting his unfortunate demise in 51, as he left rather quickly and secretly."


"Damn, does Level 9.12 even exist though?"



"That's the thing. I checked any archives of the Database list as of from when the archive was submitted to the Records Department, which was August 13th, 2022, to when the message was submitted in February of 2023."


"And, lo and behold, Level 9.12 was there, listed under the name "Dreamcore Suburbs."


"How the hell could we forget an entire level? Also, why was it deleted?"
"We have no clue, it just... vanished. From the database and from our own heads. There was no notice of the page being deleted, nor a specific reason"


"Alright, but what does this have to do with the whole Yonder situation".
"Because Level 9.12 has the same image as one of the main images Zach posted in his log. I honestly believe that Level 9.12 is the Yonder level, which they're now calling Level 434 according to the [[Phenomenon 13|Casper-Bray Numbering System]]."


"Seems like the level does not want us to know its existence."
